text: {"analysis_summary": "Reviewed the visible RTL for the debug/JTAG scope. The source contains JTAG-accessible debug interfaces that can select debug data registers and drive SoC bus-master transactions and CPU debug controls. The key permission-related issue is that the JTAG debug path shown in the inspected code has no visible authentication, lock, privilege, lifecycle, or access-policy gating before enabling debug module selection and bus/CPU operations. Comments mention password/security terms, but no implemented password check is visible. This creates a permission-control weakness: any entity able to drive the exposed JTAG pins/instructions can access debug functionality and, through the LINT/AXI debug modules, issue read/write transactions or control CPU debug signals.", "findings": [{"finding_id": "PERM-JTAG-DEBUG-UNAUTH-001", "status": "confirmed_finding", "summary": "JTAG debug access can drive bus and CPU debug functionality without visible authentication or permission gating.", "vulnerability_category": "Missing authorization / improper permission control on debug interface", "affected_locations": [{"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 11, "line_end": 15, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "signal_or_register": "PASSWORD comments / module interface"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 139, "line_end": 172, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "signal_or_register": "s_debug_select, s_shift_dr, s_update_dr, s_capture_dr, dbg_module_i"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_tap_defines.v", "line_start": 76, "line_end": 76, "module": "adbg_tap_defines", "signal_or_register": "`DEBUG"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_tap_top.v", "line_start": 507, "line_end": 507, "module": "adbg_tap_top", "signal_or_register": "debug_select"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 169, "line_end": 191, "module": "adbg_top", "signal_or_register": "module_id_reg, input_shift_reg"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 201, "line_end": 300, "module": "adbg_top", "signal_or_register": "adbg_axi_module, adbg_or1k_module, axi_master_*, cpu_stall_o"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 381, "line_end": 410, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "signal_or_register": "axi_master_aw_addr, axi_master_ar_addr, axi_master_*_prot/user/id"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 530, "line_end": 540, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "signal_or_register": "axi_master_aw_valid, axi_master_ar_valid, axi_master_w_valid"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_lintonly_top.sv", "line_start": 85, "line_end": 122, "module": "adbg_lintonly_top", "signal_or_register": "module_id_reg, input_shift_reg"}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_lintonly_top.sv", "line_start": 126, "line_end": 153, "module": "adbg_lintonly_top", "signal_or_register": "adbg_lint_module, lint_req_o, lint_add_o, lint_wen_o, lint_wdata_o"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/lint_jtag_wrap.sv", "line_start": 34, "line_end": 66, "module": "lint_jtag_wrap", "signal_or_register": "jtag_lint_master"}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/pulp_soc.sv", "line_start": 737, "line_end": 749, "module": "pulp_soc", "signal_or_register": "i_lint_jtag, s_lint_debug_bus"}], "evidence": [{"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 139, "line_end": 172, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "object": "cluster_tap_i / dbg_module_i connections", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "The debug wrapper exposes raw JTAG pad inputs and instantiates the TAP controller and debug module. The visible code connects TAP-generated debug_select and DR state signals directly into adbg_top; no authentication or permission input is shown in this path.", "supports_claim": "JTAG debug selection is directly wired to the debug module without a visible auth/permission gate."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adv_dbg_if.sv", "line_start": 11, "line_end": 13, "module": "adv_dbg_if", "object": "PASSWORD comments", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "The file contains comments mentioning PASSWORD and a fake commented constant, but no implemented password/authentication logic is visible in the searched results or module interface.", "supports_claim": "Security/password terminology appears only in comments, not as enforced permission logic."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_tap_defines.v", "line_start": 76, "line_end": 76, "module": "adbg_tap_defines", "object": "`define DEBUG 4'b1000", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "The TAP defines a DEBUG instruction value as 4'b1000.", "supports_claim": "A public JTAG instruction selects the debug path."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_tap_top.v", "line_start": 507, "line_end": 507, "module": "adbg_tap_top", "object": "`DEBUG: debug_select = 1'b1", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "When the latched JTAG instruction is DEBUG, the TAP asserts debug_select.", "supports_claim": "Selecting the DEBUG instruction directly enables debug_select."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 169, "line_end": 191, "module": "adbg_top", "object": "module_id_reg / input_shift_reg update conditions", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "adbg_top updates module_id_reg when debug_select_i, select_cmd, update_dr_i, and !select_inhibit are true, and shifts input_shift_reg when debug_select_i and shift_dr_i are true.", "supports_claim": "Debug module selection and command shifting depend on JTAG debug_select/state only; no authentication or privilege check is visible."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_top.sv", "line_start": 201, "line_end": 300, "module": "adbg_top", "object": "adbg_axi_module / adbg_or1k_module instantiations", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "adbg_top instantiates the AXI debug module and wires its AXI master outputs directly to the module outputs; it also instantiates the CPU debug module and wires CPU stall/reset/debug signals.", "supports_claim": "Once selected, the debug path can drive bus-master and CPU-control signals."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 381, "line_end": 410, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "object": "AXI address/control assignments", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "The AXI BIU drives AXI addresses from addr_reg and hardwires AW/AR protection, region, ID, user, etc. to zero values; there is no visible policy check over address or requester permission in these assignments.", "supports_claim": "JTAG-originated debug transactions become AXI transactions without visible address/permission filtering in this module."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_axi_biu.sv", "line_start": 530, "line_end": 540, "module": "adbg_axi_biu", "object": "axi_master_aw_valid / axi_master_ar_valid / axi_master_w_valid", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "The AXI BIU asserts AXI read/write valid signals in its FSM; search results show aw/ar/w valid are driven high at lines 530, 532, and 540.", "supports_claim": "The debug BIU can initiate bus operations."}, {"file": "ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl/adbg_lintonly_top.sv", "line_start": 85, "line_end": 153, "module": "adbg_lintonly_top", "object": "module select and adbg_lint_module wiring", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "adbg_lintonly_top similarly updates its module_id_reg and input_shift_reg solely from debug_select_i and JTAG DR state signals, then instantiates adbg_lint_module connected to LINT request/address/write/data outputs.", "supports_claim": "The LINT debug path is selected and commanded through JTAG without visible permission gating."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/lint_jtag_wrap.sv", "line_start": 34, "line_end": 66, "module": "lint_jtag_wrap", "object": "jtag_lint_master connections", "evidence_type": "source_snippet", "description": "lint_jtag_wrap instantiates adbg_lintonly_top and connects its lint_req/add/wen/wdata/be signals to jtag_lint_master.", "supports_claim": "JTAG debug commands drive a SoC bus master interface."}, {"file": "ips/pulp_soc/rtl/pulp_soc/pulp_soc.sv", "line_start": 737, "line_end": 749, "module": "pulp_soc", "object": "i_lint_jtag / s_lint_debug_bus", "evidence_type": "search_result", "description": "pulp_soc instantiates lint_jtag_wrap and connects jtag_lint_master to s_lint_debug_bus.", "supports_claim": "The JTAG LINT debug master is integrated into the SoC bus fabric."}], "reasoning_summary": "The visible design exposes a JTAG DEBUG instruction that asserts debug_select. That signal and TAP state signals directly control the debug top modules' shift registers and module selection. Selected debug modules can drive AXI or LINT bus-master transactions and CPU debug control outputs. Searches for authentication, secure, permission, lock, and related terms did not reveal implemented access-control logic; the only password/security references found are comments in adv_dbg_if.sv. Therefore, based on visible source evidence, debug access is not permission-gated before granting bus/CPU debug capabilities.", "security_impact": "An attacker with physical or board-level access to JTAG pins could select the DEBUG instruction and use debug commands to read or write memory-mapped assets, modify code/data, access secrets, or stall/reset/control CPU execution. If the debug bus reaches privileged registers or memory, this can become full device compromise and persistent modification depending on mapped targets.", "confidence": "high", "uncertainty_or_missing_evidence": "The analysis is limited to files and tool results visible under the input scope. It is possible that external files not included in the scope, board-level straps, lifecycle controllers, pad muxes, or downstream interconnect firewalls restrict JTAG/debug access. However, no such gating is visible in the inspected debug RTL path itself.", "recommended_follow_up": ["Add a real debug authorization mechanism, e.g. lifecycle-controlled debug enable, authenticated challenge-response unlock, fuse/OTP policy, or secure monitor-controlled gate.", "Gate debug_select_i/module_select_i and bus transaction strobes with the authorization state, not just the JTAG TAP state.", "Constrain JTAG debug bus access to approved address ranges or privilege domains, or disable write access in production.", "Ensure the authorization state resets locked and cannot be bypassed through reset, test_mode_i, or alternate JTAG/LINT wrappers.", "Review SoC interconnect/firewall files outside the visible evidence if available to determine whether downstream bus permissions mitigate this issue."]}], "no_finding_reason": "", "global_uncertainty": "Only static source snippets/search results under the provided input scope were available. Some relevant top-level integration, generated IP, pad control, fuse/lifecycle policy, or interconnect firewall implementations may be absent from the scope. No simulation, synthesis, or formal verification was performed."}
